request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose membrane stabilization.

hypothesis.raw_input: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose membrane stabilization.
hypothesis.intervention: sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.outcome: increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points

relevant_references.title: Trehalose-enhanced cryopreservation of mammalian cells (candidate, verify)
Optimization of HeLa post-thaw viability under modified cryomedia (candidate, verify)

literature_qc.explanation: Similar cryopreservation strategies are likely reported, but exact conditions appear variable.
literature_qc.recommended_action: Reuse established DMSO freezing backbone and test trehalose substitution as an adaptation.

protocol_candidates.title: Baseline DMSO freezing for adherent human cell lines (candidate)
protocol_candidates.summary: Standard controlled-rate freezing with 10% DMSO and serum-containing medium.
protocol_candidates.adaptation_notes: Replace sucrose fraction with trehalose arm while preserving cooling rate and thaw handling.

protocol_candidates.title: Trehalose supplementation in cryopreservation media (candidate)
protocol_candidates.summary: Trehalose dose exploration prior to large-batch freezing.
protocol_candidates.adaptation_notes: Run a concentration sweep pilot before committing to full-scale comparison.

evidence_claims.claim: Trehalose may improve membrane stabilization during freeze-thaw cycles.
Post-thaw viability is an interpretable primary endpoint for this cell model.
Assumed +15 percentage-point viability improvement may be optimistic.
evidence_claims.relevance_to_plan: Requires pilot threshold check before scale-up.

evidence_claims.claim: Related methods exist but differ in conditions and cell handling details.
evidence_claims.relevance_to_plan: Justifies adaptation notes and conservative controls.

risks.description: Trehalose uptake and protection may differ from expected mechanism in HeLa cells.
risks.impact: Reduced effect size despite protocol execution quality.
risks.required_mitigation: Add dose-response pilot arm and confirm osmotic tolerance.

risks.description: Cooling-rate variability can dominate cryoprotectant effect differences.
risks.impact: False attribution of viability differences to chemical composition.
risks.required_mitigation: Use controlled-rate freezing and instrument log capture for each batch.

risks.description: Single viability readout may miss delayed apoptosis after thaw.
No explicit no-cryoprotectant stress control in baseline setup.
risks.required_mitigation: Add no-cryoprotectant stress control and sham-freeze control.
Document as minimum 3 biological replicates requirement.

plan.objective: Evaluate whether trehalose substitution improves post-thaw HeLa viability against standard DMSO workflow.
plan.experimental_design: Two-arm comparative cryopreservation study with pilot concentration sweep and replicate batches.

step_by_step_protocol.description: Culture HeLa cells to consistent confluency before freezing.
Prepare control (standard DMSO) and trehalose test cryomedia arms.
Freeze using controlled-rate protocol and consistent vial fill volume.
Thaw rapidly and measure immediate viability with blinded plate layout.
Measure 24h post-thaw viability to capture delayed cell death.
Mitigation step: R1: Add dose-response pilot arm and confirm osmotic tolerance.
Mitigation step: R2: Use controlled-rate freezing and instrument log capture for each batch.
Mitigation step: R3: Include delayed 24h viability follow-up.
Mitigation step: R4: Add no-cryoprotectant stress control and sham-freeze control.

assumptions: Trehalose concentration range is compatible with HeLa osmolality tolerance.
Operator handling and thaw timing are standardized across arms.
decision_criteria: Advance if trehalose arm shows >=15 percentage-point improvement with acceptable variance.
Downgrade to pilot optimization if improvement is between 5 and 15 percentage points.
Stop if trehalose arm underperforms DMSO or increases delayed apoptosis.
risk_mitigations_applied: R1: Add dose-response pilot arm and confirm osmotic tolerance.
R2: Use controlled-rate freezing and instrument log capture for each batch.
R4: Add no-cryoprotectant stress control and sham-freeze control.

materials.name: HeLa cell line
materials.notes: Exact catalog must be verified before ordering.

materials.name: Trehalose
materials.notes: Purity grade and lot constraints to verify.

budget.notes: Costs are directional for demo use. Verify quotations before procurement.

validation.success_threshold: Trehalose arm improves 24h viability by >=15 percentage points over DMSO.
failure_conditions: Improvement <5 percentage points after three biological replicates.
Trehalose arm shows increased delayed apoptosis markers.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-sided t-test or Mann-Whitney U (depending on normality) with replicate-level analysis.

cro_ready_brief.objective: Evaluate whether trehalose substitution improves post-thaw HeLa viability against standard DMSO workflow.
scope_of_work: Run comparative cryopreservation test between standard DMSO and trehalose-modified media.
Execute pilot concentration sweep followed by replicate confirmation.
cro_ready_brief.timeline_request: Target completion in approximately 24 days including risk buffers.
questions_for_cro: Can you support controlled-rate freezing instrumentation logs?
Which viability assay kit do you recommend for 24h delayed apoptosis sensitivity?
